pattern: Treating the current root-to-node chain during a depth-first walk as a sliding window -- shrinking from the ancestor side whenever a value would occur a third time or a second value would repeat -- turns every downward special path into a window that a single traversal with careful backtracking can measure.
hints: During a depth-first walk, the sequence of ancestors from the root down to the current node is exactly one line of values; a special path ending at the current node is some suffix of that line, so track a left boundary that slides forward only when needed.

Maintain how many distinct values currently appear exactly twice and how many appear three or more times in the current window; whenever either count would become invalid, advance the left boundary and undo the ancestor values it passes over, one at a time, until the window is valid again.

Because siblings must not see each other's adjustments, remember, for every node you descend into, exactly which ancestor values your own arrival caused to be evicted and what the left boundary was beforehand, so that leaving that node's subtree can restore both precisely before moving to the next child.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, branching tree with one repeated value allowed
statement example, small tree rooted with an off-center root
smallest possible tree, a single edge
a straight line where the same value keeps repeating a third time, forcing the window to shrink repeatedly
a star-shaped tree so every path has only two nodes
all distinct values so the whole tree height is always a valid path
a wide tree where the deepest valid path is short but the shortest valid maximum-length path has few nodes

stress.description: random trees built by attaching each new node to a random earlier node, with a small value alphabet to force repeats
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 800))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        w = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        edges.append([p, i, w])
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    return [edges, nums]

stress.description: a long randomly-weighted chain to stress the iterative traversal at depth
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 20000))
    edges = [[i - 1, i, rng.randint(1, 1000)] for i in range(1, n)]
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    return [edges, nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def longestSpecialPath(self, edges, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for u, v, w in edges:
            adj[u].append((v, w))
            adj[v].append((u, w))
        freq = {}
        cntEq2 = 0
        cntGe3 = 0
        left = 0
        best_len = 0
        best_nodes = 1
        path_nodes = [0] * (n + 1)
        dist = [0] * (n + 1)
        def add_value(v):
            nonlocal cntEq2, cntGe3
            old = freq.get(v, 0)
            freq[v] = old + 1
            if old == 1:
                cntEq2 += 1
            elif old == 2:
                cntEq2 -= 1
                cntGe3 += 1
        def remove_value(v):
            nonlocal cntEq2, cntGe3
            old = freq[v]
            freq[v] = old - 1
            if old == 2:
                cntEq2 -= 1
            elif old == 3:
                cntGe3 -= 1
                cntEq2 += 1
        path_nodes[0] = 0
        add_value(nums[0])
        left_before0 = left
        evicted0 = []
        while cntGe3 > 0 or cntEq2 > 1:
            v = nums[path_nodes[left]]
            remove_value(v)
            evicted0.append(v)
            left += 1
        cur_len = dist[0] - dist[left]
        cur_nodes = 0 - left + 1
        if cur_len > best_len or (cur_len == best_len and cur_nodes < best_nodes):
            best_len, best_nodes = cur_len, cur_nodes
        stack = [[0, -1, 0, iter(adj[0]), left_before0, evicted0]]
        while stack:
            frame = stack[-1]
            node, parent, di, it, left_before, evicted = frame
            advanced = False
            for nxt, w in it:
                if nxt == parent:
                    continue
                ndi = di + 1
                path_nodes[ndi] = nxt
                dist[ndi] = dist[di] + w
                add_value(nums[nxt])
                lb = left
                ev = []
                while cntGe3 > 0 or cntEq2 > 1:
                    v = nums[path_nodes[left]]
                    remove_value(v)
                    ev.append(v)
                    left += 1
                cl = dist[ndi] - dist[left]
                cn = ndi - left + 1
                if cl > best_len or (cl == best_len and cn < best_nodes):
                    best_len, best_nodes = cl, cn
                stack.append([nxt, node, ndi, iter(adj[nxt]), lb, ev])
                advanced = True
                break
            if not advanced:
                for v in reversed(evicted):
                    add_value(v)
                remove_value(nums[node])
                left = left_before
                stack.pop()
        return [best_len, best_nodes]
